contents.value: Menu entries ------------  Most entries define a config option; all other entries help to organize them. A single configuration option is defined like this::  ```kconfig   config MODVERSIONS  bool "Set version information on all module symbols"  depends on MODULES  help    Usually, modules have to be recompiled whenever you switch to a new    kernel.  ... ```  Every line starts with a key word and can be followed by multiple arguments.  "config" starts a new config entry. The following lines define attributes for this config option. Attributes can be the type of the config option, input prompt, dependencies, help text and default values. A config option can be defined multiple times with the same name, but every definition can have only a single input prompt and the type must not conflict.
